description: Every Discovery v2 endpoint plus offline search, multi-venue watchlists, residency dedup, and on-sale tracking no API call exposes.

auth_env_var_specs.name: TICKETMASTER_API_KEY
auth_env_var_specs.description: Ticketmaster Discovery API consumer key (32-char alphanumeric).

novel_features.name: Multi-venue watchlist sweep
novel_features.description: Fan out across a venue ID file or list and return one merged, deduplicated, date-sorted event list — the watchlist primitive behind any curated 'what's on at my venues' workflow.

novel_features.name: Residency collapse
novel_features.description: Collapse runs of same-name + same-venue events into one row per residency with first_date, last_date, night_count, and id_list — so a 16-night opera season shows as one entry, not 16.

novel_features.name: Attraction tour view with on-sale flag
novel_features.description: For a given attraction (artist/team/touring show), return every upcoming event sorted by date, with city, venue, on-sale status, and a flag for events going on-sale within 7 days.

novel_features.name: On-sale-soon scanner
novel_features.description: Local query for events whose public on-sale falls in the next N days, sorted ascending — the canonical 'presale watch' view that no API endpoint provides.

novel_features.name: Classification bucketing
novel_features.description: Local join of events × classifications, grouped by segment and genre, with event count and three example events per leaf — the bucketed view newsletter authors and local-scene trackers reach for.

novel_features.name: Named persistent watchlists
novel_features.description: Save, list, run, and remove named filter sets (venue IDs, attraction IDs, segments, DMA IDs) that persist across runs in the local SQLite store — the generic primitive any curated 'my venues' workflow composes from.

novel_features.name: Stream-shaped dedup
novel_features.description: Read an event JSON array from stdin or the local store, apply a deduplication strategy (name+venue+date, or tour-leg), and write the deduped stream to stdout — composes with any upstream command.

novel_features.name: Markdown what's-on brief
novel_features.description: Render a markdown 'what's on' report grouped by night → venue → events with classification labels and price bands, suitable for newsletter, Obsidian, iMessage, or agent context.

novel_features.name: Price-band distribution
novel_features.description: Bucket events by priceRanges.min into <$50 / $50-100 / $100-200 / $200+ bands and report count + sample events per band, grouped by classification.
